metadata.title: Operation Dream Job Energy Sector Attack Scenario
metadata.author: Cybersecurity Analyst

Scenario.overview: Energy Simple Operation Dream Job attackers target energy sector employees with basic skills using spearphishing and drive-by compromise techniques to gain initial access, execute malicious activities, and exfiltrate sensitive data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Hacking Tools, Scheduled Tasks
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, PowerShell Script, Scheduled Task/Job, Command Execution (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends targeted phishing emails to energy company employees, tricking them into revealing sensitive information.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker compromises employee devices through malicious websites or attachments.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-108: Command Line Execution through SQL Injection
stage.description: The attacker uses PowerShell scripts for lateral movement within the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker creates scheduled tasks for persistent access.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker steals sensitive data related to energy infrastructure.

Scenario.summary: Operation Dream Job attackers use spearphishing and drive-by compromise to gain initial access, then leverage PowerShell scripts and scheduled tasks for execution and impact.
